words.definition_en: to make a bad situation worse
words.example: His inflammatory comments are just aggravating an already difficult situation.

words.definition_en: to be or do something better than someone or something else
words.example: I set the school record eight years ago and it's never been bettered.

words.definition_en: a mark or stain that spoils the appearance of something or someone
words.definition_vi: vết nhơ, khiếm khuyết (trên bề mặt)
words.example: The face in the picture is angelic, except for that tiny blemish just below the eye.

words.definition_en: a state of total confusion and lack of order
words.example: The rock festival was fun, but it was marred by the organisation, which was a chaos.

words.definition_en: to make someone or something seem less valuable or respected
words.definition_vi: làm rẻ rúng, làm mất giá trị
words.example: Pop songs have cheapened the word 'love' so that it's meaningless nowadays.

words.definition_en: to make something dirty, polluted, or poisonous by adding a chemical, waste, or infection
words.example: A lorry carrying chemicals has crashed into a river and has contaminated the local water supply.

words.definition_en: to be gradually destroyed by a natural process
words.example: Sugar makes your teeth decay.

words.definition_en: to become less or worse
words.example: The number of school-leavers going on to university has declined since the government introduced tuition fees.

words.definition_en: not working correctly because of a fault
words.example: This CD-ROM drive isn't working properly; it must be defective.

words.definition_en: harmful or damaging
words.example: I think the government's measures are going to do quite a lot of damage; they are detrimental to the economy.

words.definition_en: to cause great damage or suffering to something or someone
words.definition_vi: tàn phá, phá hủy hoàn toàn
words.example: The town was devastated by the hurricane.

words.definition_en: to improve something
words.example: This new browser should enhance everyone's experience online.

words.definition_en: to think carefully about something before making a judgment about its value, importance, or quality
words.example: The inspectors are going to be evaluating the performance of every teacher.

words.definition_en: to make a problem become worse
words.example: The Minister's inflammatory comments are just exacerbating an already difficult situation.

words.definition_en: extremely beautiful and delicate
words.example: I've never seen a more beautiful diamond! It's exquisite.

words.definition_en: of the highest quality
words.example: Excellent work. Well done! It's first-rate.

words.definition_en: a mistake or fault in something that makes it less than perfect
words.example: The plan has several serious flaws.

words.definition_en: perfect, or the best possible
words.example: This is exactly what I need; it's the ideal solution.

words.definition_en: not enough or not good enough for a particular purpose
words.example: The current security measures are inadequate.

words.definition_en: extremely useful
words.definition_vi: vô giá, cực kỳ hữu ích
words.example: The job couldn't have been done without your help; your advice was invaluable.

words.definition_en: the best or most suitable amount or level of something
words.example: Four is the right number of people for this project; it's the optimum number.

words.definition_en: to be much better than someone or something else
words.example: The visiting team completely outclassed the home side.

words.definition_en: most important or of the highest quality
words.example: This was the most important reason for us; it was our prime motivation.

words.definition_en: a positive quality which improves something that is otherwise bad
words.definition_vi: điểm vớt vát, ưu điểm bù đắp
words.example: His honesty is the only feature about him that's positive; it's his only redeeming feature.

words.definition_en: to improve a room or a building by cleaning and painting it, adding new furniture, etc
words.example: The hotel has been completely refurbished.

words.definition_en: to make an idea or a structure stronger
words.example: The results of the survey reinforce my belief that we need to change.

words.definition_en: to repair and decorate a building that is old and in bad condition
words.example: They spent thousands of pounds renovating the house.

words.definition_en: decayed
words.example: This apple's completely black; it's rotten.

words.definition_en: covered with rust
words.example: These metal railings are really old and brown; they are rusty.

words.definition_en: good enough to be accepted in a particular situation
words.example: The price seems reasonable to me. I'll take it. It's satisfactory.

words.definition_en: a situation in which there is a lot of confusion or lack of organization
words.definition_vi: tình trạng hỗn độn, đống lộn xộn
words.example: The rock festival was fun, but it was marred by the organisation, which was a shambles.

words.definition_en: shoddy work, services, or products are of a very low standard
words.example: The workmanship on this cabinet isn't very good; it's shoddy.

words.definition_en: involving or based on good judgment
words.example: I think it was a sensible decision; it was sound advice.

words.definition_en: stale food such as bread or cake is no longer fresh or good to eat
words.example: This bread's not very fresh; it's stale.

words.definition_en: to improve a business, organization, or process by making it more modern or simple
words.example: The procedure's good, but I think we can streamline it further to make it more efficient.

words.definition_en: to make something stronger
words.example: We need to strengthen the bridge.

words.definition_en: to be better or greater than something else
words.example: We must never let inflation surpass the three per cent mark again.

words.definition_en: happening at the end of a long process; or the best or most extreme example of something
words.example: It's the most exciting experience you'll ever have! The ultimate experience.

words.definition_en: to become worse, or to make something worse
words.definition_vi: làm tệ đi, trở nên tệ hơn
words.example: This accident has worsened Dagli's chances of competing in the final.

words.definition_en: to severely damage something; or a vehicle or building that has been badly damaged
words.definition_vi: phá hỏng, tàn phá; đống đổ nát
words.example: The ship was a total wreck.

words.definition_en: abstract art expresses ideas and emotions by using shapes and colours rather than representing people or things
words.example: The catalogue will help you work out what the artist was trying to express in these abstract paintings.

words.definition_en: a public occasion when things are sold to the people who offer the most money for them
words.example: You don't have to be rich to attend an auction.

words.definition_en: a short performance that an actor, musician, etc. gives in order to show they are suitable for a job
words.example: I've got an audition for a part in a play.

words.definition_en: a book that many people buy
words.example: Her latest novel became a bestseller within weeks of being published.

words.definition_en: a rare or valuable object that people want to collect
words.example: On any one day at the auction, there may be collector's items.

words.definition_en: someone whose job is to look after the objects in a museum or art gallery
words.definition_vi: người phụ trách bảo tàng/phòng tranh
words.example: We got chatting to the curator, who told us an interesting story about one picture.

words.definition_en: objects such as paintings and sculptures that are created to be looked at because they are beautiful or interesting
words.example: The course covers both fine art and graphic design.

words.definition_en: a piece of art that consists of several different objects or pictures arranged to create a particular effect
words.example: Galleries often display bizarre installations, such as a pile of bricks.

words.definition_en: the words that an actor must learn for a particular part in a play or film
words.example: I've learnt all my lines for the play.

words.definition_en: the words of a song
words.example: I'm just no good at remembering lyrics.

words.definition_en: an excellent painting, book, piece of music, etc., or the best work that a particular artist, writer, or musician has ever produced
words.example: The Mona Lisa is da Vinci's masterpiece.

words.definition_en: a book with a soft cover
words.example: I'm waiting for the book to come out in paperback.

words.definition_en: typical of a particular historical time
words.definition_vi: thuộc về một thời kỳ lịch sử
words.example: The catalogue includes period furniture from the 18th century.

words.definition_en: too valuable to be given a price
words.example: In fact, the painting turned out to be priceless!

words.definition_en: a performance of music or poetry
words.definition_vi: buổi độc tấu, buổi ngâm thơ
words.example: The school orchestra's giving a recital tonight.

words.definition_en: an exhibition that includes examples of a particular artist's work from their whole career
words.definition_vi: triển lãm hồi tưởng (nhìn lại sự nghiệp)
words.example: We went to that retrospective of Lewis' paintings last week.

words.definition_en: the music written for a film or play
words.example: He's written the score for a number of movies.

words.definition_en: a simple picture that is drawn quickly and does not have many details
words.example: On any one day, there may be sketches in pencil.

words.definition_en: something such as a painting or sculpture that is of very high quality
words.example: Restoring damaged or faded works of art must be really satisfying.

words.definition_en: not having any value, or not useful
words.example: He thought the picture was worthless and gave it to a neighbour.

words.definition_en: start to have more colour or light; start looking or feeling happier; (weather) become sunnier
words.definition_vi: làm sáng sủa hơn, vui vẻ hơn
words.example: Luckily, the weather brightened up in the afternoon.

words.definition_en: practise and improve your skills or knowledge of something
words.definition_vi: ôn tập, trau dồi lại kiến thức
words.example: You'll have to brush up on your general knowledge before you go on that quiz show.

words.definition_en: examine someone or something to be certain that everything is correct; see what something is like
words.example: I'm going to check it out.

words.definition_en: make something more interesting or exciting, or become more interesting or exciting
words.definition_vi: làm cho sống động, hào hứng hơn
words.example: The party livened up when Rod got out his guitar.

words.definition_en: change or improve the appearance of someone or something
words.definition_vi: thay đổi diện mạo, tân trang
words.example: We're going to completely make over the spare bedroom.

words.definition_en: make a mistake or do something badly; make something dirty or untidy
words.example: I think I've messed up the perspective a little.

words.definition_en: hide a problem or disagreement rather than finding a satisfactory solution to it
words.example: They have papered over their differences while they get their business set up.

words.definition_en: repair something quickly/temporarily; become friends again after a disagreement; give basic medical treatment
words.definition_vi: vá víu, hàn gắn (mối quan hệ)
words.example: I've only patched this up for now.

words.definition_en: improve
words.example: If attendance doesn't pick up soon, the play will probably have to close.

words.definition_en: if an organisation or area is run down, its size, importance and activity is reduced
words.example: The previous administration had run the film industry down.

words.definition_en: succeed in doing something, but not in a very impressive way
words.definition_vi: vừa đủ điểm đậu, vượt qua khó khăn
words.example: Susan scraped through her history of art course.

words.definition_en: improve the appearance of something; make yourself look tidy and clean
words.definition_vi: làm cho gọn gàng, bảnh bao hơn
words.example: We always smartened ourselves up when our grandparents came to visit.

words.definition_en: be much more impressive or important than others; be easy to see or notice
words.example: One of the poems particularly stands out.

words.definition_en: make a surface look better with small improvements
words.definition_vi: chỉnh sửa, dặm lại (cho đẹp hơn)
words.example: I'll scan it and touch it up with some software.

words.definition_en: gradually become thinner and weaker over a period of time, usually because of an illness
words.example: I almost wasted away!

words.definition_en: damage a vehicle so badly it's not worth repairing; decide someone/something won't succeed and stop giving attention
words.definition_vi: xóa bỏ, coi như hỏng hoàn toàn
words.example: The car was so badly damaged it had to be written off.

words.definition_en: someone or something that you are very pleased to see
words.definition_vi: người/vật mà mình rất vui khi gặp lại
words.example: You're a sight for sore eyes, Claire! Thank goodness you're here.

words.definition_en: a weak feature of someone or something that could cause failure or be attacked
words.definition_vi: điểm yếu chí mạng (gót chân Achilles)
words.example: His one Achilles' heel, however, may be his lack of business sense.

words.definition_en: make a bad situation worse
words.example: Allegations have only added fuel to the fire regarding speculation about his future.

words.definition_en: below the usual or expected standard
words.definition_vi: dưới mức tiêu chuẩn, không tốt như mong đợi
words.example: Amis' new novel is slightly below par for a writer of his stature.

words.definition_en: completely honest or legal; extremely clean
words.example: I've spent the whole morning vacuuming the car. It's now clean as a whistle.

words.definition_en: say that you will definitely not allow or accept something
words.definition_vi: đưa ra giới hạn, không chấp nhận việc gì
words.example: I'm happy to simplify things, but I draw the line at telling blatant untruths.

words.definition_en: the newest and best type of something
words.example: Soccer Maestro is undoubtedly the last word in online football simulation games.

words.definition_en: extremely good or impressive
words.definition_vi: tuyệt vời, không còn gì để chê
words.example: The buffet at the party was out of this world!

words.definition_en: more than what is considered normal or suitable
words.example: I think the media's response has been a little over the top.

words.definition_en: in the place that is most central or important
words.example: The picture that takes pride of place on our mantelpiece is of our granddaughter.

words.definition_en: receive a lot of attention because you give the best performance
words.definition_vi: giành hết sự chú ý (vì trình diễn quá hay)
words.example: All the bands were fantastic, but Franz Ferdinand really stole the show.

words.definition_en: an advantage that makes someone or something more successful than others
words.example: The Gordon Gallery clearly has the edge over other modern art galleries.

exercises.label: A
exercises.instruction: Circle the correct word. If both are correct, circle both.

items.sentence: The procedure's good, but I think we can ___ it further to make it more efficient.
items.explanation: 'Streamline' means to improve a process by making it simpler and more efficient.

items.sentence: The shell of the building has been completely ___ and the interior decorators will be starting inside next week.
items.explanation: Both 'renovate' and 'refurbish' can mean to repair and improve a building.

items.sentence: The Minister's inflammatory comments are just ___ an already difficult situation. He should think before he opens his mouth next time.
items.explanation: Both verbs mean to make a bad situation worse.

items.sentence: The inspectors are going to be ___ the performance of every teacher.
items.explanation: 'Evaluate' means to assess quality or performance; 'value' usually means to estimate monetary worth.

items.sentence: In Berkshire, a lorry carrying chemicals has crashed into a river and has ___ the local water supply.
items.explanation: 'Contaminate' means to make something dirty or poisonous; 'decay' is a natural process of rotting.

items.sentence: I set the school record for the 100m over eight years ago and it's never been ___ to this day.
items.explanation: To 'better' a record means to achieve a better result than it.

items.sentence: This new browser should ___ everyone's experience online.
items.explanation: 'Enhance' means to improve the quality or value of something.

items.sentence: The number of school-leavers going on to university has ___ since the government introduced tuition fees.
items.explanation: 'Decline' means to decrease in number or quality.

items.sentence: We must never let inflation ___ the three per cent mark again.
items.explanation: 'Surpass' means to exceed a certain level or amount.

items.sentence: This accident has ___ Dagli's chances of competing in the final.
items.explanation: 'Wreck' means to destroy completely; 'worsen' means to make worse. Both could fit, but 'wrecked' is more idiomatic for destroying chances.

items.sentence: Pop songs have ___ the word 'love' so that it's meaningless nowadays.
items.explanation: 'Cheapen' means to make something seem less valuable or respected.

items.sentence: The rock festival was fun, but it was marred by the organisation, which was a ___.
items.explanation: 'A shambles' is a noun phrase used to describe a very disorganized situation. 'Chaos' is usually uncountable.

items.sentence: The face in the picture is angelic, except for that tiny ___ just below the eye - can you see it? - that seems to be some kind of scar or birthmark.
items.explanation: A 'blemish' is a small mark that spoils an appearance.

exercises.label: B
exercises.instruction: Circle the word which best matches each statement.

items.sentence: 'I've never seen a more beautiful diamond!'
'His honesty is the only feature about him that's positive.'
items.explanation: A 'redeeming feature' is a positive quality in something otherwise bad.

items.sentence: 'These metal railings are really old and brown.'
'It's just not good enough.'
'The workmanship on this cabinet isn't very good.'
items.explanation: 'Shoddy' describes work of very low standard.

items.sentence: 'This CD-ROM drive isn't working properly.'
items.explanation: 'Defective' means not working correctly due to a fault.

items.sentence: 'This apple's completely black.'
'It's just the most important reason for us.'
'The job couldn't have been done without your help.'
'The price seems reasonable to me. I'll take it.'
'This bread's not very fresh.'
items.explanation: 'Stale' describes bread that is no longer fresh.

items.sentence: 'I think the government's measures are going to do quite a lot of damage.'
'It's the most exciting experience you'll ever have!'
items.explanation: 'Ultimate' means the best or most extreme example.

items.sentence: 'I think it was a sensible decision.'
'This is exactly what I need.'
'Excellent work. Well done!'
'Four is the right number of people for this project.'
items.explanation: 'Optimum' means the most suitable amount or level.

exercises.label: C
exercises.instruction: Write a word from the boxes in each gap.

items.sentence: I haven't read Susan Bashworth's latest yet but I've heard it's very good. It became a ___ within weeks of being published.
items.explanation: A 'bestseller' is a very popular book.

items.sentence: I'm actually waiting for it to come out in ___ before I get it.
items.explanation: A 'paperback' is a book with a soft cover, usually released after the hardback.

items.sentence: I've learnt all my ___ for the play, which was easy...
...but we have to sing a song at the end and I'm just no good at remembering ___.
items.explanation: The words of a song are called 'lyrics'.

items.sentence: The school orchestra's giving a ___ tonight which sounds quite interesting.
They've got a guest conductor who's quite famous. Apparently, he's written the ___ for a number of movies.
items.explanation: A 'score' is the music written for a film.

items.sentence: We went to that ___ of Lewis' paintings at the Granchester Museum last week.
items.explanation: A 'retrospective' is an exhibition of an artist's career work.

items.sentence: We got chatting to the ___ , who told us a very interesting story about one picture ...
items.explanation: A 'curator' is someone who manages a museum or gallery collection.

items.sentence: He told us that someone had found it in their attic, thought it was ___ and so gave it to a neighbour.
In fact, it turned out to be ___ !
One of the ___ I'd love to do is restoring damaged or faded...
items.explanation: Refers to a profession or task.

items.sentence: ...___ of art.
items.explanation: 'Works of art' is the standard term for artistic creations.

exercises.label: D
exercises.instruction: Complete using the words in the box in the correct form. There is one word you will not need.

items.sentence: When most of us think of works of art, we tend to think of pieces in galleries, whether they be famous ___ (such as da Vinci's Mona Lisa) hanging on the wall...
...or bizarre ___ (such as a pile of bricks with a red shoe on top).
items.explanation: 'Installations' are modern art pieces made of objects arranged in a space.

items.sentence: Another way is to go to a/an ___ .
items.explanation: A public sale where art is sold to the highest bidder.

items.sentence: On any one day, there may be ___ in pencil (artists generally do these before painting the actual picture)...
...___ art (which includes sculptures and ceramics as well as paintings)...
items.explanation: 'Fine art' refers to traditional visual arts.

items.sentence: ...collector's ___ (anything from broomsticks to postcards)...
items.explanation: 'Collector's items' are objects sought by collectors.

items.sentence: ...___ paintings (the catalogue will help you work out what the artist was trying to express)...
items.explanation: 'Abstract' art does not represent literal objects.

items.sentence: ...and even ___ furniture.
items.explanation: 'Period' furniture belongs to a specific historical time.

exercises.label: E
exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap so the second sentence has a similar meaning to the first sentence.

items.sentence: You'll have to ___ up on your general knowledge before you go on that quiz show.
items.explanation: 'Brush up on' means to improve or refresh your knowledge.

items.sentence: The car was so badly damaged it had to be ___ off.
items.explanation: 'Write off' a vehicle means it's beyond repair.

items.sentence: One of the poems particularly ___ out due to its interesting use of imagery.
items.explanation: 'Stand out' means to be very noticeable or impressive.

items.sentence: If attendance doesn't ___ up soon, the play will probably have to close.
items.explanation: 'Pick up' means to improve or increase.

items.sentence: I've heard a new gallery has opened in St Ives, so I'm going to ___ it out.
items.explanation: 'Check something out' means to examine or visit it.

items.sentence: Susan ___ through her history of art course.
The photo's got a couple of marks on it, so I'll scan it and ___ it up with some software I've got.
items.explanation: 'Touch up' means to make small improvements to the appearance.

items.sentence: On the show today, we're going to completely ___ over the Simpsons' spare bedroom.
items.explanation: 'Make over' means to renovate or improve the appearance.

exercises.label: F
exercises.instruction: If a word is in the wrong sentence, write the correct word on the line. If it is correct, put a tick.

items.sentence: The party was a bit boring to start with but everyone livened up when Rod got out his guitar.
I'm not very pleased with this drawing actually as I think I've papered up the perspective a little.
I've only wasted this up for now - I won't be able to fix it properly until we get home.
Julie and Simone have messed over their differences while they get their business set up...
The Prime Minister responded that it was the previous administration which had patched the film industry down.
Luckily, the weather brightened up in the afternoon so we were able to hold the reception outside after all.
As kids, we always ran ourselves up when our grandparents came to visit.
The holiday at the health farm was awful... I almost smartened away!
I want this mess cleared up right now. Did I ___ myself clear?
It's ___ and warm in here.
Off the top of my ___ , I'd say this diamond's worth at least a million euros.
items.explanation: Idiom: 'off the top of your head'.

items.sentence: You're cutting it ___ . The meeting starts in about two minutes.
items.explanation: Idiom: 'cut it fine' (allowing barely enough time).

items.sentence: To the ___ of my knowledge, there aren't any Picassos on the market at the moment.
items.explanation: Phrase: 'to the best of my knowledge'.

items.sentence: That Ferrari Craig's just bought is ___ new. It must have cost him a fortune!
Lee's his own worst ___ . He complains he hasn't sold any of his paintings, but he won't show them to anyone.
There's been bad ___ between them ever since Trevor accused Charlton of sabotaging his opening night.
I'm not going to do her dirty ___ for her any more.
My mum's got organising her Christmas card list round ___ to a fine art.
items.explanation: Idiom: 'have something down to a fine art'.

exercises.label: H
exercises.instruction: For each question, write one word which can be used in all three sentences.

items.sentence: We had to make the ___ of a rather disastrous situation. / Do your ___ in the exam. / It's probably for the ___ .
items.explanation: Phrases: 'make the best of', 'do your best', 'for the best'.

items.sentence: I'm afraid we're ___ out of that CD. / I'm determined to make a ___ start. / And now, ___ from their blockbuster tour...
She wants to make a ___ break. / The doctors have given me a completely ___ bill of health. / You all start again with a ___ sheet.
items.explanation: Phrases: 'clean break', 'clean bill of health', 'clean sheet'.

items.sentence: Is long hair back in ___ ? / I actually don't know much about that ___ of architecture. / I'd be treated with ___ wherever I went.
items.explanation: Phrases: 'in style', 'style of architecture', 'treated with style'.

items.sentence: I bumped into an ___ flame of mine. / Dave's an ___ hand. / putting Mum in an ___ people's home.
I'm not going to let them get the ___ of me. / camp in the grounds, ___ yet. / I'm certainly all the ___ for two weeks in the sun.
items.explanation: Phrases: 'get the better of', 'better yet', 'all the better for'.

exercises.label: I
exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: There is a widely held view that artistic standards have got ___ over the last 100 years or so...
...and that contemporary art is ___ a bad way...
How can we get to the ___ of this seeming contradiction?
items.explanation: Idiom: 'get to the bottom of something'.

items.sentence: Those new ___ contemporary art should be forgiven...
What needs to be ___ clear is that contemporary art is at the cutting edge...
...we would actually have the ___ of all worlds - a stagnant and backward-looking artistic tradition.
Having said that, some contemporary art is of poor ___ , but that's true of every art...
...___ at any point in history.
However, those ___ the top of the contemporary art world are producing innovative and challenging pieces.
At ___ best, contemporary art is as exciting and productive as it's possible for art to be.
There is, of course, a ___ line between quality contemporary art and pseudo-art.

exercises.label: J
exercises.instruction: Complete using the words in the box.

items.sentence: Soccer Maestro is undoubtedly the last ___ in online football simulation games at the moment.
The picture that takes ___ of place on our mantelpiece, though, is the one of our granddaughter graduating.
You're a ___ for sore eyes, Claire!
Jean Paul is clearly a very talented designer. His one Achilles' ___ , however, may be his lack of business sense.
The buffet at the party was out of this ___ !
Amis' new novel is slightly below ___ for a writer of his stature...
...but The Editors and Franz Ferdinand really stole the ___ .
I'm happy to simplify things for the readers, but I draw the ___ at telling them blatant untruths.
I think the media's response has been a little over the ___ - it's only a TV programme, after all.
Recent allegations... have only added ___ to the fire regarding speculation about Dotheby's future.
It's now clean as a ___ .
The Gordon Gallery clearly has the ___ over other modern art galleries...

exercises.label: K
exercises.instruction: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the space.

items.sentence: Well, she did study ___ at Oxford, so it's hardly surprising she knows Latin and Ancient Greek.
items.explanation: 'Classics' is the study of ancient Greek and Roman culture.

items.sentence: The Pet Shop Boys' sound is ___ and unique.
What this painting says to me is that we live in a world of ___ and despair.
I've got enormous ___ for Ben; he's achieved such a lot in such a short space of time.
The main reason I believe children shouldn't be exposed to violence on TV is that they're so ___ at that age.
I'm a bit of a ___ , so I can spend all day agonising over which choice of two words to use.
items.explanation: A person who wants everything to be perfect.

items.sentence: Maybe I am an ___ ; but what's wrong with wanting every human being to have access to clean drinking water?
items.explanation: A person who believes in ideal principles.

items.sentence: I've read that many performers suffered feelings of ___ as children.
items.explanation: 'Inadequacy' is the feeling of not being good enough.

items.sentence: I don't know how ___ it would be to get some of these old postcards valued.
items.explanation: 'Worthwhile' means useful or important enough to spend time on.

items.sentence: We'll find out how much the jewellery's worth at the ___ next Thursday.
items.explanation: 'Valuation' is the process of estimating the monetary value.

items.sentence: Now the government's ___ the papers, we can find out what really happened.
items.explanation: 'Declassify' means to officially state that information is no longer secret.

items.sentence: Being director of the National Gallery is an ___ responsibility but thankfully I have a number of highly experienced experts to help me.
items.explanation: 'Awesome' here means very impressive or daunting.

items.sentence: With her experience as both an actor and a director, Tabitha is a very ___ successor to Albert Weeks...
There were some very interesting clay ___ dating from the third century BC at the museum.
items.explanation: 'Artefacts' (or artifacts) are historical objects made by humans.

items.sentence: Performing on stage for the first time was the most ___ experience I've ever had.
Most of it's ___ if you use a little creativity.
There's a ___ difference between photography and still-life drawing.
items.explanation: 'Qualitative' relates to the quality or standard rather than quantity.

exercises.label: L
exercises.instruction: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the space.

items.sentence: ...I had to trawl through hundreds of ___ (CLASS) ads each week...
items.explanation: 'Classified ads' are small advertisements in newspapers or online.

items.sentence: ...Auction sites like eBay, though, are extremely ___ (IMPRESS)...
...and a great ___ (IMPROVE).
It's just ___ (TERROR) what you can do at the click of a button.
items.explanation: 'Terrifying' here means shockingly impressive or slightly scary in its speed/power.

items.sentence: I generally buy and sell ___ (COLLECT) items.
items.explanation: 'Collectable' (or collectible) items are sought by collectors.

items.sentence: ...plastic ___ (ART) flowers.
items.explanation: 'Artificial' means made by people, not natural.

items.sentence: ___ (COLLECT) are interested in the strangest things...
...often because they're ___ (USE) and still in their packaging...
...glassware that might have ___ (PERFECT).
One of my ___ (STRONG) is that I've got a very good rating...
I'm also good at spotting what's ___ (VALUE)...
...and what's completely ___ (WORTH).
For example, here's a pair of ___ (MATCH) earrings.
...can tell immediately they're ___ (IMITATE).
Diamond's basically ___ (DESTROY), you see...
Ideally, it's better to actually see the ___ (GOOD) you're buying...

exercises.label: M
exercises.instruction: The prefix in- can often mean 'not' or 'no'. However, in words such as invaluable, in- does not mean 'not' or 'no'. Tick (V) the words where it means 'not' or 'no'. Put a cross (X) next to the words where it doesn't.

items.sentence: The inbound flight from Chicago...
I really do value my independence.
The whole family was infamous round here...
items.explanation: 'Infamous' means NOT having a good reputation (though colloquially 'well known for something bad').

items.sentence: ...because of all the infighting.
This material is inflammable, isn't it?
items.explanation: 'Inflammable' means easily set on fire (same as flammable), not 'not flammable'.

items.sentence: What an ingenious idea.
items.explanation: 'Ingenious' comes from Latin 'ingenium' (innate ability), not 'not genious'.

items.sentence: The place will soon be inhabitable, won't it?
items.explanation: 'Inhabitable' means ABLE to be lived in, not 'not habitable'.

items.sentence: The whole area feels very inhospitable.
The situation is intolerable.
She was charged with being disorderly and intoxicated.
items.explanation: 'Intoxicated' means under the influence of toxins/alcohol, not 'not toxicated'.
